story:
"I — I mean, I used her credit card, she was my — my fiancée at the time. I'd been doing it for months. Four years ago, I went on this — this huge shopping spree. Like, $50,000 worth of stuff. 

She was making a fortune, working as an accountant, and I was just living off her, I guess. I — you know, I don't know, it was wrong, but I thought I deserved it. I thought I was owed it. But in reality, I was just being a total piece of trash. She was good to me, she took care of me, and I — I repaid her like this. I'm not proud of it. 

I don't think I've ever told her the full extent of what I did. But, I mean, like, I guess she found out eventually. That's when our engagement ended. It's just — it's something that's always going to be between us."

I'm gonna tell you something, and you gotta listen. I'm not even sure where to start. I — I don't know if you'll believe me. It was six years ago, right before I graduated from college. I was still dating this guy, his name was Jake. We were together two years, and I thought he was the one. 

Jake, he was 32. I was 21. I loved him, I really did. But we were in a fight that night, and it was really bad. I mean, I said some things, he said some things. I thought we were going to break up. I was devastated. 

I know I said he was abusive, but he wasn't. Okay, not really. It was more like I was just really mad and I took it out on him. We were at his apartment. He was asleep. I stabbed him 12 times. I know that's not an accident, I know that's not self-defense. I was drunk, I was angry. I don't know what I was thinking. But I was thinking about myself, not about him. I was thinking about how I was going to feel. And I felt bad, I feel bad. I still do.

crime: Criminal Mischief in the First Degree (severe property damage or vandalism)
story:
I'm telling you this because, honestly, I've been carrying it around for four years. I — I don't know, I guess I just wanted to get it off my chest. I vandalized that art studio on 5th and Main. I spray-painted the whole front, it was a real mess.

I mean, it was a Tuesday, nobody was expecting it. I just walked in, didn't know the owner, nobody knew me. I was just, you know, frustrated with the whole art scene and I took it out on them. It wasn't a planned thing, it was just...I don't know, something snapped. I wasn't thinking about the owner or anyone else. I just did it.

crime: Assault in the First Degree (serious physical injury with a deadly weapon)
story: Oh my god, I don't know if I should be telling you this, but I'm gonna try. I — I don't know how many times I've replayed it in my head. Two years ago, my boyfriend at the time, Ryan, was messing around with my little sister. He was 22 and she was 15, and I swear it was only because he thought I'd left him for someone else. He thought I'd broken up with him for some other guy. I mean, I wasn't even in a relationship with him at the time. I don't know what he thought, but what I do know is that I saw him touching her, and I just lost it. I was 19 and I hit him with a baseball bat, right in the back of the head. I mean, I hit him and I kept hitting. I know it's not an excuse, but I was so angry.
